per_entity_results.generated: QUIC Implementation Benchmarking and Energy Measurement Across Multiple Nodes
per_entity_results.reference: Performance Evaluation of QUIC Implementations with Limited Bandwidth

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset documents a series of experiments evaluating the performance of several QUIC implementations. The methodology involved orchestrating client and server processes on multiple nodes, configuring various network bandwidth limits and file sizes, and measuring energy consumption per run. Evaluation scripts aggregated timing and hash values to generate visualizations of goodput performance, with per-run logs and evaluation outputs archived for analysis. The dataset includes scripts for build environment setup, network configuration, and data collection.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment executes a single file download with client-server pairs of different QUIC implementations. The filesize, the implementations, and a bandwidth limit can be set to multiple values to execute the measurements with all combinations of the parameters. The amount of repetitions can also be set to repeat the same combination n times to ensure stable results. An inbuilt evaluation script aggregates results and creates a simple plot that compares different QUIC implementations.

per_entity_results.generated: This script orchestrates a server process, configuring network bandwidth limits and launching a QUIC server implementation selected from a predefined list. It generates test content, manages certificates, and synchronizes with a client process using pos-based signaling. Per-run logs and energy measurements are captured and uploaded.
per_entity_results.reference: Start a QUIC server with a given library. In advance, a testfile and a certificate is generated. If set, a bandwidth limit is applied with Linux tc. After a client signalizes a finished transfer, the server is terminated.

per_entity_results.generated: Each iteration of the script orchestrates a server, varying parameters such as implementation, bandwidth_limit, and filesize, synchronizing with a client, and uploads per-run logs.
per_entity_results.reference: In each iteration, the QUIC client downloads a testfile from the QUIC server. All combinations for all values of implementation, bandwidth and filesize are run at least once, depending on the amount of repetitions set. For each run, the duration as well as logs from the respective endpoint are stored.

per_entity_results.generated: This script orchestrates client-side benchmark runs across multiple QUIC implementations, synchronizing with a server and capturing energy measurements per run. per_entity_results.reference: Start a QUIC client to download a file from a QUIC server. After a complete download, the file is hashed and the resulting value is stored to a file.
